Treasurer religiously issue demand letters to the granted beneficiaries to enforce
collection; coordinate/ask assistance from other government offices, COMELEC
for instance, where current registered addresses of the debtors might be
available.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to:
• exert effort to verify the Source Agencies of the various receipts and
remit/revert all unexpended/dormant balances, especially the PDAF, to
the source agency/general fund or Bureau of Treasury so that it can help
augment the finances for other priority projects/programs of the national
government or other agencies. Thereafter, effect the necessary
adjustments/journal entries in the books of account;
• retrieve and evaluate all the documents pertaining to the unpaid
payables, determine the legitimacy of the transactions and if found to be
valid claims, communicate with the concerned payees for the
payment/settlement of the accounts. Otherwise, revert to the
Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund those that proved to be
with no legal claims.

We recommended that the funds received from National Government
Agencies (NGAs) be utilized fully for the benefit of the constituents and ensure
the proper use thereof in accordance with the guidelines under the DBM-DILG-
DSWD-NAPC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 7 and the respective projects’
Memorandum of Agreement.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
1) instruct the BAC to submit justification and explanation why the supplier was
considered as an eligible bidder even though the latter was not able to submit
NFCC or CLC as required by the provision of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A.
No. 9184;
2) require the Supply Officer to prepare Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) to
support the issuance of 114 pieces knapsack sprayer, 754 sacks of organic
fertilizer (Vermicast) and vegetable seeds; and
3) require the Municipal Agriculturist to submit to COA the list of the
beneficiaries/recipients with their addresses and
acceptance/acknowledgement of the goods.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards
Committee to submit a written justification why the submitted Tax Clearances of
the declared bidders were not validated before the eligibility process and during
the post-qualification process, thus, resulting in the award of contracts. Otherwise,
enforce strictly the sanctions against the erring contractors, BAC/TWG and other
concerned officials for lapses committed on ensuing procurements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe the proper accounting
of funds received from National Government Agencies and other funding
institutions in future similar transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
and the MSWD Officer to complete the required information in the submitted
payrolls in order to validate the authenticity and accuracy of the paid payrolls
amounting to ₱3,382,500.00. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant and the
MSWDO to ensure completeness of the required supporting documents in the
disbursements of the funds under the Social Pension Program.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• form or establish a Solid Waste Management Board in accordance with
the requirements provided under Section 12 of RA No. 9003 to ensure
effective and efficient formulation and implementation of the
Municipality’s Solid Waste Management Plan;
• encourage and support the establishment of Multi-Purpose Environment
Cooperatives or Association to help promote the implementation of RA
N. 9003; and
• thru the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (MSWMB) require
strict implementation of all Municipal Ordinances and continue the
information drive specifically related in environmental compliance to
ensure proper waste segregation, thorough collection of waste based on
set schedules and reduction of wastes.

We recommended that the Management direct the members of the LCPC
to prepare Local Development Plan for Children and Local Investment Plan for
Children, and maximize utilization of the allocated LCPC Fund to identified
PPAs provided for in DILG MC No 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012 to ensure full
strengthening and implementation of the LCPC. Inform the Sanggunian Bayan
Members on the PPAs that can be funded by the 1% IRA for the strengthening
of LCPC.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Development
Council clearly specify the detailed descriptions, locations and cost of priority
programs, projects and activities funded out of 20% Development Fund and
ensure that such projects partake the nature of investment or capital
expenditures. For projects that are presented in generic terms and with lump-
sum appropriation, secure the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan before the
implementation and disbursements of the fund.

b. A total of ₱796,042.91 was utilized from the 20% Development Fund for non-
developmental activities contrary to DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2017-1, thereby reducing the benefits that could be derived
therefrom had the fund been utilized strictly as intended.
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to avoid charging
expenditures, though contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic
development and environmental management outcomes but, however do not
take part the nature of investment or capital expenditure.

c. Lapses/deficiencies in the procurement process enumerated hereunder were
noted in the procurement process of thirty (30) infrastructure projects funded
from the 20% Development Fund with total contract amount of ₱17,443,192.11
in violation of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 thereby raising doubts
whether the principles of transparency, accountability, equity, efficiency, and
economy of the procurement process were fully achieved.
We recommended the Municipality’s adherence to the rules and regulations
set forth in R.A. No. 9184 or the Government Procurement Reform Act and to its
Revised IRR so as to promote transparency, accountability, equity, efficiency and
economy in the procurement process to achieve good governance.
We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct members of the Bids
and Awards Committee to check, verify and validate all the eligibility documents
including the tax clearance certificates submitted by the contractors before
awarding contracts to bonafide contractors. Otherwise, enforce strictly the
sanctions against the erring contractors, BAC/TWG and other concerned officials
for lapses committed on ensuing procurements.
We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer perform planning and
148

We recommended that the LCE strengthen the MDRRMC by completing the
required membership of the Council.

We recommended that the Municipality thru its MDRRMC fully implement the
MDRRMF programs and see to it that all expenses incurred were in accordance
with the approved budget.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the the
concerned Municipal officials to ensure that disbursements out of the fund are
those that only pertain to disaster risk and management activities provided by
Joint Circular 2013-1.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
Municipal Budget Officer to discontinue the practice of including MOOE as
part of the Continuing Appropriations.

We recommended that the Supply Officers of the Municipality and Bauan General Hospital:
a) secure a copy of the PPE Ledger Cards or the Schedule of Depreciation Expenses from the Accounting Unit which can be the basis in the preparation of PPE inventory list by preparing Property category and the Property Cards.
b) religiously perform the actual physical count of PPE by asking assistance from all department heads and accountable personnel to conduct inventory count of the properties on their accountability and generate the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) following the prescribed format in Sec. 57 of NGAS Manual on LGUs, Volume II.
c) compare the results with the Schedule maintained by the Accounting Unit. Differences, if any, should be identified and traced whether these are transfers, missing or unserviceable.
d) evaluate the intention of the municipality on the transfers made to other agencies. If the transfer is permanent, substantiate such with proper documents prescribed by Section 381 of Local Government Code and Sections 185 and 186 of Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management in Local Government prescribed by COA Circular 92-386.
e) investigate on the cause of the missing or unlocated properties. Identify who are accountable by tracing the recipients from the Memorandum Receipt (MR) or the Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE). The accountable officer should request for the relief from property accountability if the loss was not due to his/her negligence but if found that he did not perform due care as that of a good father, he/she should be held liable for the replacement cost of such property or equipment. Refer to Sections 151 and 152 of COA Circular 92-386 on the requirements for application for relief, if applicable.
f) for the unserviceable properties, have an updated list of those items for disposal, prepare all the necessary documents/reports following Sections 162 to 168 prescribed per COA Circular No. 92-386 and COA Circular 89-296 to act on the properties to be disposed within reasonable time.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal and Bauan General Hospital (BGH) Supply Officers commented that lack of manpower hindered the timely completion of the physical count of PPEs aside from non-coordination of the different departments with their offices in the conduct of the physical count. The Municipal and Bauan General Hospital (BGH) Supply Officers, and the Municipal Accountant agree to reconcile their records to present fairly the balances of the PPEs in the books of the municipality.
Nevertheless, the management was amenable with the observations and expressed their commitment to comply with the other recommendations.
observations.reason: The bulk of the PPE accounts and lack of manpower. The management is still in the process of beginning with the latest acquisitions. (Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 1, AAR Part II, Page No. 39)

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant on her letter dated May 08, 2018 commented that the cash deficit under Trust Fund amounting to ₱10,399,666.35 was due to the unexpended Quick Response Fund (QRF) and Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) of Disaster Risk Reduction Management (DRRM) Fund amounting to ₱9,459,982.47 which was recognized as receivable from General Fund for the year 2017. This was transferred on March 20, 2018 under OR No. 21826558 and 21826559 amounting to ₱3,580,308.97 and ₱5,879,673.50, respectively. The difference of ₱939,683.88 is an old balance which resulted from the reclassification of accounts in compliance with the New Government Accounting System (NGAS).
observations.reason: The municipality still incurred cash overdraft in the Trust Fund books amounting to ₱10,399,666.35. (Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 2, AAR Part II, Page No. 47)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Engineer submit their justification for the differences noted in the computation of OCM, Profit and VAT amounting to ₱7,858,509.08. Further, reconcile the discrepancy of ₱45,641.88 in the computation of detailed estimate.
observations.managementAction:
The management submitted already their justification.
On the discrepancy of ₱45,641.88 in the computation of detailed estimate, they have traced such to the cost of aluminum composite panels for Item No. 13.
observations.reason: No year-end inventory was provided to the Municipal Accountant; however the management has submitted their justification.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
a. the Municipal Budget Officer to discontinue the practice of including MOOE as part of the Continuing Appropriations. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer properly monitor and review the reported charges against appropriations and ascertain the accuracy of the details reflected in the Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations and other reports.
b. the Municipal Accountant to transfer the remaining unexpended balance of MOOE and Quick Response Fund to the Special Trust Fund and religiously comply with proper accounting for the recognition of the receivable from General Fund in the Trust Fund books if cash transfer is not yet possible.
c. the concerned municipal officials to ensure that disbursements out of the fund are those that only pertain to disaster risk and management activities provided by Joint Circular 2013-1.
d. the members of the BAC-Technical Working Group to explain why the existence of non-authentic Tax Clearances despite their mandate to verify, validate all supporting documents. e. the Municipal Accountant to explain the discrepancy in the dates appearing in the disbursement attendance sheets of the attendees in the Oplan Semana Santa versus other related documents.
f. the concerned municipal officials to cause the refund of ₱48,400.00 expense incurred in CY 2015 but not included among the accounts payable and paid in CY 2016.
g. Strictly impose on the contractors the sanctions provided under Section 69 of Rule XXIII of the IRR of RA 9184 for misrepresentation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant conferred during the exit conference that they will be requesting from some of the Source Agencies to allow them to use the remaining balance of the funds for other programs/projects/activities while the other receipts will be reverted back to the Source Agencies.
observations.reason: Unexpended balance for MOOE of the 70% Mitigation Fund was recognized as receivable in the Trust Fund books while other actions such as transfer of some balances to Trust Fund were effected; non-DRRM expenses totaling ₱74,659.02 were still charged to LDRRMF in CY 2017. (Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 17, AAR Part II, Page No. 106)

observations.recommendation:
a. We recommended that the Management exert more efforts in locating the records pertaining to the outstanding receivable balance of the “Study Now, Pay Later Program” in order to substantiate its existence and make possible the recovery of the said receivables. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer religiously issue demand letters to the granted beneficiaries to enforce collection. Coordinate/ask assistance from other government offices, COMELEC for instance, where current registered addresses of the debtors might be available.
b. We recommended that the Management review the charging of expenses made particularly those which were recorded as expense but should have been capitalized being directly attributable to a PPE item and effect appropriate adjustments to arrive at the correct balance of PPE account.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant commented that the related documents for the mentioned dormant accounts can no longer be located. As for the receivables under the “Study Now, Pay Later Program,” the management assured the team that they will continuously send demand letters to the beneficiaries and coordinate with other government offices to trace the latters’ addresses.
Correcting entry had been made per JEV No. 2001707248 dated July 31, 2017 recording such construction in SEF.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer issued demand letters to the granted beneficiaries of “Study Now, Pay Later Program” to enforce collection. However, only ₱41,903.35 was collected. (Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 10, AAR Part II, Page No. 71)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to continuously coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to reconcile the balances of the reciprocal accounts Due from/Due to LGUs by identifying the causes of discrepancies in the corresponding subsidiary ledgers for adjustment, if warranted. Likewise, effect the immediate settlement of obligations of each fund for a fair presentation of the cash position of each fund.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant explained on her letter dated April 30, 2018 that the discrepancy between Due from LGUs (Province) per municipal books and Due to LGUs (Municipality) per provincial books, the difference of ₱294,811.06 resulted from double posting of barangay share amounting to ₱71,696.97 under General Fund and Trust Fund on December 2017 and failure to adjust the municipality’s share on SET accruing on December 2016 amounting to ₱223,114.09. These were corrected under JEV No. 10018030367 and JEV No. 2001803095, respectively.
She further added that the discrepancy between Due to LGUs (Province) per municipal books and Due from LGUs (Municipality) per provincial books was due to timing difference. The provincial shares on real property taxes for December 2017 amounting to ₱72,388.52 for General Fund and ₱102,665.73 for Special Education Fund were remitted on December 29, 2017 under check no. 59738627 with JEV No. 10017122853 and check no. 54316933 with JEV No. 2001712438, respectively, however these were received by the provincial government on January 2018 only. The remaining balance of ₱194,410.82 in the General Fund and ₱194.15 in the Trust Fund were mere old balances in the provincial books which are due for write-off since no records can be found in the municipal books.
observations.reason: ₱194,410.82 and ₱194.15 were old balances on the Provincial Books which will be requested for write-off since the LGU has no records of which in its books. (Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 8, AAR Part II, Page No. 68)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant:
- to record purchases of office supplies, accountable forms, drugs and medicines, medical and dental laboratory supplies, food supplies, gasoline, oil and lubricant expense and other supplies expense as debit to appropriate asset account using the perpetual inventory system regardless of whether they were consumed within the accounting period;
- to maintain the subsidiary ledger cards for each supplies and materials; and
- to record the issuances base on the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI).
b. Municipal and Bauan General Hospital Supply Officers:
- to maintain stock cards and property cards for supplies;
- to prepare and submit monthly the RSMI to the Municipal Accountant for the recording of issuances; and
- to prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to report the physical count of supplies by type of inventory as of a given date.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Supply Officer discussed in his letter dated April 10, 2018 that the lack of available spacious stockroom, proximity of other offices, and failure to inform the Supply Unit on a regular basis for the supplies and materials purchased by the different offices in the LGU hamper the LGU’s compliance with the abovementioned regulations.
observations.reason: No year-end inventory was provided to the Municipal Accountant; lack of adequate stockroom; supplies distributed immediately to requisitioning departments and not recorded via RIS/SSMI.

observations.recommendation:
On Transfer of Funds from National Government Agencies (NGAs) for its Regular Projects:
a. We recommended that the Municipality refrain from disbursing government funds without the necessary supporting documents. Submit the lacking documents like Certificate of Indigency, Clinical Abstract and Certificate of Approval from the Chief of Hospital. Refund the total amount of ₱13,093.95 as overpayment to Bauan General Hospital.
b. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to compute for the liquidated damages and collect the amount from the concerned contractor in relation to approvals of extension of contract time for the National Child Development Center; and other appropriate measures.
c. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the related agencies to require proper documentation and monitoring for grants like four-wheel tractor, movable power sprayer and 1,000 units organic fertilizer to ensure beneficiaries actually received and to require accreditation/certification from concerned agencies as appropriate.
d. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC to require the contractors to access and use the PhilGEPS and BAC Secretariat to upload walk in bidders and to strictly adhere with the pertinent provisions of RA 9184 in posting the Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and Contract Agreement and that the Municipal Engineer prepare the Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) in accordance with DPWH D.O. 22 Series of 2015.
e. Strictly impose on the contractors the sanctions provided under Section 69 of Rule XXIII of the IRR of RA 9184 for misrepresentation.
observations.managementAction:
a. The management submitted copies of Certificate of Indigency, Clinical Abstract and Certificate of Approval from the Chief of Hospital. A Statement of Account (SOA) from Bauan General Hospital for Mr. Cesar Bernabe was submitted showing a total bill of ₱187,793.63 instead of ₱6,906.05. The management explained the lack of approval by the Chief of Hospital on the request for medical assistance was out of respect to the Municipal Mayor who is the overall head even in Bauan General Hospital since it is operated by the municipality.

b. The management submitted their justification. c. The BAC Chairman replied that consideration of the lone bidder who failed to comply with the eligibility requirement of CLC, in lieu of NFCC computation, was an honest mistake and was done in good faith and assured the Audit Team that they will be more vigilant in screening documents. d. The management conferred that they are encountering system problems with PhilGEPS thus posting of Notice of Award cannot be done accordingly. However, they are now fully compliant on the posting of the Notice of Award to PhilGEPS. e. The management imposed already the sanction provided under Section 69 of Rule XXIII of RA 9184 to the abovementioned erring contractors through letters dated August 29 and September 22, 2017.
observations.reason: Some procurement documents and PhilGEPS postings were not completed at time of audit; certain sanctions were imposed; some procurements completed prior to issuance of earlier AOM. (Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 13, AAR Part II, Page No. 82)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Development Council clearly specify the detailed descriptions, locations and cost of priority programs, projects and activities funded out of 20% Development Fund and ensure that such projects partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures. For projects that are presented in generic terms and with lump-sum appropriation, secure the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan before the implementation and disbursements of the fund.
We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant avoid charging expenditures, though contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes but, however do not take part the nature of investment or capital expenditure.
We also recommended that the members of the Bids and Awards Committee to check, verify and validate all the eligibility documents including the tax clearance certificates submitted by the contractors before awarding contracts to bonafide contractors. Otherwise, enforce strictly the sanctions against the erring contractors, BAC/TWG and other concerned officials for lapses committed on ensuing procurements.
observations.managementAction: The management commented that they will be specifying the PPAs for the 20% Development Fund. The management explained that all the PPAs to be charged under the 20% Development Fund are now in accordance with the DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2017-1. The management is now more cautious in verifying, validating and checking the eligibility requirements of the bidders. The BAC has enjoined sanctions to erring contractors as provided under Section 69 of RA 9184.
observations.reason: Attempts to comply with DILG-DBM Joint Circular; some non-developmental charges still noted (₱796,042.91) charged to 20% DF; some tax clearance issues were earlier sanctioned. (Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 16, AAR Part II, Page No. 96)

observations.managementAction: The management has submitted their justification. The management submitted all the lacking documents stated in the 2016 AAR.
observations.reason: No amended contract was submitted to the Audit Team; however the management provided the needed justifications and supporting documents where available. (Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 10, AAR Part II, Page No. 97)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin:
a. the members of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to recommend appropriate method of procurement after a thorough study/evaluation and encourage competitive bidding instead of alternative modes when circumstances allow and avoid splitting of purchases. Further, require the supplier/s to specify the brand of drugs and medicines delivered in the delivery receipt and sales invoice for verification purposes.
b. the end-user units to prepare a detailed PPMP, properly and completely filled out and signed by proper authorities. Further, update the PPMP as the need arises. Moreso, adopt a reasonable unit cost in making estimates.
c. the BAC Secretariat to review and consolidate PPMPs of end-user units into an APP including the PPMP of Bauan General Hospital. Likewise, utilize the APP to systematically manage the procurement processes and see to it that all the procurement is in accordance with the APP. Also, cause the posting of Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and the approved contract in the PhilGEPS or the website of the procuring entity, if any.
d. the members of the Bids and Awards Committee and the BAC Technical Working Group to explain why the winning bidder was declared eligible when the submitted Tax Clearance is not recognized by Accounts Receivable Monitoring Division of the Bureau of Internal Revenue. Likewise, the BAC and TWG members shall check, verify and validate all the eligibility documents including the tax clearance certificates submitted by the contractors before awarding contracts to bonafide suppliers.
e. strictly impose on the contractors the sanctions provided under Section 69 of Rule XXIII of the IRR of RA 9184 for misrepresentation.
observations.managementAction: For the last three quarters of CY 2017, competitive bidding was done in the purchase of drugs and medicines. The LGU is now preparing a detailed PPMP. The BAC has been reminded to validate eligibility requirements of every contractor. Sanctions have been imposed to erring suppliers.
observations.reason: Prior lapses noted; subsequent quarters showed competitive bidding and improvements; some documentation issues remained but management acted to comply.

observations.managementAction: The Municipality had re-organized its GAD Focal Point per Administrative Order No. 15 Series of 2017 dated August 30, 2017 effective immediately. The LGU submitted GPB for CY 2017 to the Provincial Planning and Development Office which reviewed and endorsed it to DILG. The Municipal GFPS underwent GAD seminar/training/capability building on November 20-22, 2017 at Bayview Hotel, Manila. The LGU has established GAD Database in the first quarter of CY 2018 and the Municipal Budget Officer promised to allot 1% of their Share from IRA for LCPC and include at least 5% for GAD in yearly budgets.
observations.reason: GAD Focal Point re-organized; GAD database institutionalized in CY 2018; some GAD activities not yet fully mainstreamed and some non-GAD programs were charged to GAD fund; GPB review pending final DILG endorsement.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board oversee the implementation and review every two (2) years or as the need arises the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan for purposes of ensuring its sustainability, viability, effectiveness and relevance in relation to local and international developments in the field of solid waste management; b. the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant adopt measures and controls that will promote reliability of payments made for the garbage disposal at sanitary landfill; submit justification on the utilization of garbage trucks appearing in the bill which were not driven by the official drivers of the Municipality; c. the MENRO adopt reliable measurement of the attainment of the set goals/targets in the 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan in order to enjoin all stakeholders in areas that need improvements; d. the Municipal Agriculturist revive the operation of the vermi-composting in coordination with the Department of Agriculture and farmers’ associations; e. Barangay officials to adopt specific revenue-generating measures to promote the viability of the Solid Waste Management Plan in their respective barangay and ensure such measures are not costly on the part of the constituents and will not compromise its internal control; f. strictly implement all enacted ordinances especially the segregation of wastes at source by households and different institutions and establishments; g. continue the dissemination of information on ordinances adopted regarding solid waste management to various residents and establishments; and h. the concerned municipal officials to submit the lacking documents as prescribed by COA Circular 2012-001 to substantiate payments made by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The LGU really try to implement RA 9003 through the Solid Waste Management Plan. The LGU purchased two (2) trucks, one (1) with bin lifter and three (3) brand new closed bin which are being used for segregation especially in the market area and municipal hall. The LGU will try its best to implement RA 9003 in the years to come. Segregation at source is implemented at Bauan Public Market. Administrative Order No. 22 dated May 24, 2018 re-organized the MSWMB incorporating a junkshop owner as a representative from the recycling industry. The LGU conducted Eco-Summit and IEC implementation. The Municipal Engineer and MPDC submitted justifications where applicable. The LGU provided explanations relative to hauling contracts and use of LGU-owned trucks.
observations.reason: The Comprehensive Solid Waste Management ordinance was enacted on June 27, 2005 but not strictly discharged; some actions such as procurement of trucks and IEC campaigns were implemented; full segregation at source across all barangays not yet achieved.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
1. the Municipal Treasurer provide the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of each year with a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as basis for the setting-up of receivable; Likewise, submit to the Sangguniang Bayan certified list of all real property tax delinquencies which remained uncollected or unpaid for at least one (1) year and a statement of the reason or reasons for such non-collection or non-payment on or before December thirty-first (31st) of the year immediately succeeding the year in which the delinquencies were incurred, with a request for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for collection;
2. the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile their records to present an accurate and reliable RPT and SET Receivables in the financial statements; and
3. the Municipal Mayor, as well as the Sangguniang Bayan Members, adopt measures to collect from delinquent taxpayers and strengthen and intensify collection system and tax campaign to minimize tax delinquencies.
observations.managementAction: During the exit conference, the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant promised to reconcile their records while the SB Secretary commented that a taskforce was already created for the enforcement of collection of tax delinquencies. The Municipal Treasurer added that the LGU had submitted the Statement of Account indicating the tax delinquencies to the Provincial Government and the Municipal Assessor explained that the province conducts an auction sale yearly. The Municipal Accountant reconciled amounts in her records.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer will reconcile their records; some actions such as demand letters and coordination with provincial government and taskforce creation were undertaken but full collection and reconciliation still ongoing. (Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 5, AAR Part II, Page No. 56)

observations.managementAction:
On her letter dated May 07, 2018, the Municipal Accountant replied that to avoid liquidation beyond the required period, strict monitoring of the cash advances will be implemented. The grant to officers and employees exceeding their maximum cash accountability will be minimized with their enrollment to Digital Banking of the Development Bank of the Philippines (DBP). Further, cash bond to be granted to officers and employees upon renewal will be reviewed to cover their total accountability.
For CY 2017, no cash advance was granted to elected officials for purposes other than travel. Some accountable officers refunded amounts where applicable and explanations were provided for noted inconsistencies.
observations.reason: Although the monitoring will be implemented, few municipal officials and employees failed to adhere to COA Circular No. 97–002 in previous periods; some refunds were made and justifications submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to submit justification why several non-GAD programs and activities were charged to the fund instead of charging them in the regular General Fund.
We recommended that the Municipality thru its GAD Focal Point System plan and implement projects and programs that will directly benefit women. Consider the potentials and borne skills of women in the formulation of strategies and bases that will be used in designing priority projects and programs for GAD Plan responsive to its prescribed thrust and vision.
We recommended also that the GAD Focal Point Team strictly monitor the implementation of the planned GAD program, projects and activities to ensure that the plan was fully implemented.
observations.managementAction:
The management had already submitted Sangguniang Bayan Resolution No. N-083 dated August 22, 2016 which approves Ordinance No. 16-07-0102 dated July 25, 2016 entitled “An ordinance granting financial assistance to the constituents of this municipality, prescribing corresponding amount, enumerating qualifications, requirements and appropriating funds thereof.”
The management has submitted refunds where double claims were evident and required officials to refund. The LGU now ensures compliance with permitted expenditures.
observations.reason: Payments were reviewed and refunds requested where double claims were identified; ordinance providing financial assistance was adopted to guide future grants.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Sangguniang Bayan to establish clear cut guidelines on the selection and grant of AICS aligned with the DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 02, series of 2014; the SB Secretary to refund the amount which was claimed twice for the same Official Receipt; the Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from expending beyond the budget and observe the correct posting in the Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Continuing Obligations to properly monitor the balances to avoid overspending; the Municipal Accountant to submit the documents required for its release and check the completeness of related transactions.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO submitted Memorandum Circular No. 2014-02 dated February 17, 2014 as their legal basis for the amount of AICS granted to beneficiaries. The Sangguniang Bayan passed resolution and ordinance prescribing qualifications and appropriations for financial assistance. The Municipal Accountant gathered documents required for release and checked completeness where available.
observations.reason: Sangguniang Panlalawigan Resolution No. N-083 and Ordinance No. 16-07-0102 were issued; the registry and posting issues were to be addressed; some documents for CY 2015 release could no longer be provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for the operation of the LGU’s economic enterprises like market and slaughterhouse, Bauan General Hospital, etc. and prepare the yearend financial statements such as Statement of Financial Performance, Statement of Financial Position and Statements of Cash Flows and supporting schedules of the General Fund Trial Balance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant explained that the preparation of these reports is still on-going.
observations.reason: Difficulty to retrieve data impedes the preparation of financial statements for the special accounts. (Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 7, AAR Part II, Page No. 66)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make proper representation with the BIR and GSIS to determine with finality any obligation existing with the said government agencies. If such action provide adequate information as to the validity and accuracy of said unpaid obligation, then immediate remittance of the balances be undertaken to rid the books of unsettled obligations. Otherwise, if said undertaking revealed that the BIR and GSIS have no outstanding collectibles from the Municipality, then prior approval of the Municipal Council is required before any reversion could be made of the unremitted amount.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant explained that the extent of liability rests with the Mayor, Accountant and Treasurer. Surcharges and penalties that would be imposed on the old balances when computed by the BIR would be shouldered by them.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant indicated representation will be made to BIR/GSIS; some old balances to be addressed via remittance or write-off as appropriate.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Supply Officers of the Municipality and  Bauan
General Hospital:

  a) secure a copy of the PPE Ledger Cards or the Schedule of  Depreciation
     Expenses from the Accounting Unit  which  can  be  the  basis  in  the
     preparation of PPE inventory list by category and the Property Cards.

  b) religiously perform  the  actual  physical  count  of  PPE  by  asking
     assistance from all department  heads  and  accountable  personnel  to
     conduct inventory count of the properties on their accountability  and
     generate the Report  on  the  Physical  Count  of  PPE  following  the
     prescribed format in Sec. 57 of NGAS Manual on LGUs, Volume II.

  c) compare the results with the Schedule  maintained  by  the  Accounting
     Unit.  Differences, if any, should be identified  and  traced  whether
     these are transfers, missing or unserviceable.

  d) evaluate the intention of the municipality on the  transfers  made  to
     other agencies. If the transfer is permanent, substantiate  such  with
     proper documents prescribed by Section 381 of  Local  Government  Code
     and Sections 185 and 186  of  Rules  and  Regulations  on  Supply  and
     Property Management in Local Governments prescribed by COA Circular 92-
     386.

  e) investigate on the cause  of  the  missing  or  unlocated  properties.
     Identify who are  accountable  by  tracing  the  recipients  from  the
     Memorandum Receipt (MR) or the Acknowledgement Receipt  for  Equipment
     (ARE).  The accountable officer should request  for  the  relief  from
     property accountability if the loss was not due to his/her  negligence
     but if found that he did not perform  due  care  as  that  of  a  good
     father, he/she should be held liable for the replacement cost of  such
     property or equipment. Refer to Sections 151 and 152 of  COA  Circular
     92-386 on the requirements for application for relief, if applicable.

  f) for the unserviceable properties, have an updated list of those  items
     for disposal, prepare all the  necessary  documents/reports  following
     Sections 162 to 168 prescribed per COA Circular No.  92-386   and  COA
     Circular 89-296 to  act  on  the  properties  to  be  disposed  within
     reasonable time.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

        a. Local Finance Committee to adopt strategy in order  to  increase
           revenue/collections and monitor  properly  the  availability  of
           cash every month to ensure that all income due the  Municipality
           be collected in full and assure continuous  flow  of  funds  and
           that the trust funds received be utilized only for the  specific
           purpose for which it was created;

        b. the Municipal Treasurer to prepare Daily Cash Position Report to
           serve as guide to him in certifying that funds are available  to
           ensure that all disbursements have cash back up;


        c. Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal  Accountant,  and  Municipal
           Treasurer to exercise diligence, prudence and proper control  in
           the  disposition  of  the   Municipality’s   funds   and   limit
           expenditures within the  total  income  realized  and  collected
           during the year to avoid incurrence of cash overdraft.

        d. the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to  explain
           why the Trust Fund was utilized and  disbursed  other  than  its
           intended purpose contrary to Section 309  (b)  of  RA  7160  and
           Section 84 of PD 1445, thus,   resulted  in  the  incurrence  of
           cash overdraft amounting to ₱30,856,963.83 in the Trust Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor  and  the  Municipal  Engineer
submit justification for the differences noted in the  computation  of  OCM,
Profit  and  VAT  amounting  to  ₱7,858,509.08.   Further,   reconcile   the
discrepancy of ₱45,641.88 in the computation of detailed estimates.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:

  a. the Municipal Budget Officer to discontinue the practice of  including
     MOOE as part of the Continuing Appropriations. Likewise, we  recommend
     that the Municipal Budget Officer  properly  monitor  and  review  the
     reported charges against appropriations and ascertain the accuracy  of
     the details reflected in the Registry  of  Appropriations,  Allotments
     and Obligation and other reports.

  b. the Municipal Accountant to transfer the remaining unexpended  balance
     of MOOE and  Quick  Response  Fund  to  the  Special  Trust  Fund  and
     religiously comply with proper accounting for the recognition  of  the
     receivable from General Fund in the Trust Fund books if cash  transfer
     is not yet possible.

  c. the concerned Municipal officials to ensure that disbursements out  of
     the fund are those that only pertain to disaster risk  and  management
     activities  provided  by  Joint  Circular   2013-1   and   that   each
     disbursement is supported with proper attachments.

  d. the members of the BAC-Technical Working  Group  to  explain  why  the
     existence of non-authentic  Tax  Clearances  of  RTM  Development  and
     Construction inspite their mandate to verify, validate all eligibility
     requirements submitted by the contractors before awarding contracts to
     bonafide suppliers.

  e. the Municipal Accountant to  explain  the  discrepancy  in  the  dates
     appearing in the attendance sheets  of  the  attendees  in  the  Oplan
     Semana Santa versus other related documents.

  f. the concerned municipal officials to cause the  refund  of  P48,400.00
     expense incurred in CY  2015  but  not  included  among  the  accounts
     payable and paid in CY 2016.

  g. Strictly impose  on  the  contractors  the  sanctions  provided  under
     Section 69 of Rule XXIII of the IRR of RA 9184 for misrepresentation.

The management had responded that the Municipal Treasurer has  already
drafted a demand letter for distribution to the  beneficiaries  relative  to
the Other Receivables account amounting to ₱7,776,899.11  arising  from  the
implementation of “Study Now, Pay Later Program”.

      Correcting entry had been made per JEV No. 2001707248 dated  July  31,
2017 recording such under Other Structures account  relative  to  the  major
improvement of covered court at  Manalupang-San  Vicente  Elementary  School
which has been recorded as expense.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  instruct  the  Municipal
Accountant to continuously coordinate  with  the  Provincial  Accountant  to
reconcile the balances of the reciprocal accounts Due from/Due  to  LGUs  by
identifying the causes of  discrepancies  in  the  corresponding  subsidiary
ledgers for  adjustment,  if  warranted.   Likewise,  effect  the  immediate
settlement of obligations of each fund for a fair presentation of  the  cash
position of each fund.

Management Comment:

      During the exit conference, the management  expressed  willingness  to
comply with the audit team’s recommendation.

We recommended that the Municipality   refrain   from   disbursing
government funds without the  necessary  supporting  documents.  Submit  the
lacking documents like Request for Medical Assistance approved by the  Chief
of Hospital, Certificate of  Indigency,  Request/Doctor’s  Prescription  and
Medical Abstract. Refund the total amount of ₱13,093.95  as  overpayment  to
Bauan Doctor’s General Hospital.

      Likewise, we recommended that the Chief of Hospital  explain  why  the
medical assistance was disbursed without his approval contrary to Section  C
of DOH Administrative Order No. 2015-005.


      We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

     a) the BAC to submit justification and  explanation  why  the  supplier
        was considered as an eligible bidder  when  the  submitted  committed
        line  of  credit  was  not  issued  by  a  commercial  or  universal
        bank as required by the provision of the IRR of RA 9184.

     b)  require  the  15  farmer  associations   through   the   Municipal
        Agriculturist to submit the respective accreditation from  DOLE  or
        any other related government agencies to validate the legitimacy of
        the associations.

     c) require the Municipal Agriculturist to justify the distribution  of
        the  organic  fertilizers  only  among  the   Presidents   of   the
        Associations  instead  of  the  individual  farmer   beneficiaries.
        Likewise, provide a report on the distribution of the 1,000  pieces
        fertilizers.

     d)  instruct  the  Municipal  Agriculturist  to  prepare   a   regular
        monitoring report for  all  beneficiaries  especially  the  farmers
        borrowing the four-wheel tractor.  Devise  a  concrete  policy  and
        mechanisms for approval by the Municipal Mayor wherein the LGU  can
        monitor the usage and borrowing schemes of the  four-wheel  tractor
        to ensure that no government funds will be wasted.

     e) instruct the Municipal Accountant to  ensure  completeness  of  the
        required supporting documents in the disbursements of the funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:

   a.  the  Municipal  Development  Council  to  clearly  specify  the  detailed
      descriptions, locations and cost of priority  programs,  projects  and
      activities funded out of 20 percent Development Fund and  ensure  that
      such  projects  partake  the   nature   of   investment   or   capital
      expenditures.  For projects that are presented  in  generic  terms  and
      with lump-sum appropriation, secure  the  approval  of  the  Sangguniang
      Bayan before the implementation and disbursements of the fund;


   b. the  Municipal  Budget  Officer  and  Municipal  Accountant  to  avoid
      charging  expenditures,  though  contribute  to  the   attainment   of
      desirable  socio-economic  development  and  environmental  management
      outcomes but, however do not take part the  nature  of  investment  or
      capital expenditure; and


   c. the members of the Bids and Awards  Committee  to  check,  verify  and
      validate all the eligibility documents  including  the  tax  clearance
      certificates submitted by the contractors before awarding contracts to
      bonafide suppliers.

   d. strictly impose  on  the  contractors  the  sanctions  provided  under
      Section 69 of Rule XXIII of the IRR of RA 9184 for misrepresentation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin:

   a)  The  members  of  the  Bids  and  Awards  Committee  to  recommend
      appropriate   method    of    procurement    after    a    thorough
      study/evaluation.  Encourage   competitive   bidding   instead   of
      alternative  modes,  when  the  circumstances  allows   and   avoid
      splitting of purchases. Further, require the supplier/s to  specify
      the brand of drugs and medicines delivered in the delivery  receipt
      and sales invoice for verification purposes.

   b) The end-user  units  to  prepare  a  detailed  PPMP,  properly  and
      completely filled out and signed by  proper  authorities.  Further,
      update the PPMP as the need arises. Moreso, adopt a reasonable unit
      cost in making estimates.

   c) The BAC Secretariat to review and  consolidate  PPMPs  of  end-user
      units into an APP including the PPMP  of  Bauan  General  Hospital.
      Likewise, utilize the APP to systematically manage the  procurement
      processes and see to it that all the procurement is  in  accordance
      with the APP. Also, cause the posting of Notice of Award, Notice to
      Proceed and the approved contract in the PhilGEPS or the website of
      the procuring entity, if any.

   d) the members of the Bids and Awards Committee and the BAC  Technical
      Working Group  to  explain  why   declare  the  winning  bidder  as
      eligible when the submitted Tax  Clearance  is  not  recognized  by
      Accounts Receivable Monitoring Division of the Bureau  of  Internal
      Revenue. Likewise, the BAC and TWG members shall check, verify  and
      validate all the eligibility documents including the tax  clearance
      certificates submitted by the contractors before awarding contracts
      to bonafide suppliers.

   e) Strictly impose  on  the  contractors  the  sanctions  provided  under
      Section  69  of  Rule  XXIII  of   the   IRR   of   RA   9184   for
      misrepresentation.

Management Comment:

      The management commented that although they failed to implement  waste
segregation at source  in  every  household,  the  garbage  collectors  upon
collection segregate the solid waste; recyclables turned into junkshops  and
final segregation is  done  at  the  landfill.  Moreover,  the  Solid  Waste
Management Board  that  will  oversee  the  implementation  of  Solid  Waste
Management Plan was only created last December 15, 2016 by AO No. 23. As  of
now, the segregation of waste at source is being done at the public market.

      On the utilization of garbage trucks, the  Engineering  Office  issued
certification justifying the  use  of  other  borrowed  private  trucks  for
garbage collection when LGU’s trucks become  unoperational.  The  management
also commented that although it is the duty of the  driver  and  helpers  to
ensure that the trucks are being disinfected per trip, some still failed  to
comply.
      The MENRO together with the Solid  Waste  Management  Board  will  re-
assess on how the  set  goals  and  targets  in  the  10  year  Solid  Waste
Management   Plan   will   be  100%   attained.   The   LGU   said   that   the
“no segregation no collection policy”.  The LGU will conduct IEC to all
stakeholders for them to understand the RA 9003.

       The  Municipal  Agriculturist  has  ongoing  coordination  with   the
Department Agriculture to  revive  the  operation  of  vermin-composting  in
Brgy. San Teodoro hence, they  said  that  if  this  will  materialize,  the
biodegradable waste in the market will be converted to fertilizer.

      The LGU through its ABC  Office  will  conduct  Information  Education
Campaign (IEC) to every barangay to make sure  that  the  Material  Recovery
Facility (MRF) they established will be operational.

      The LGU had conducted  Eco-Summit  to  all  establishment  located  in
Bauan regarding the implementation of RA 9003 last December  2016.  The  LGU
has ongoing Information Education Campaign at the  public  market  regarding
waste segregation at source as part of RA 9003  implementation,  spearheaded
by the DRRM Office in coordination  with  the  MENRO  and  MPDC.  They  have
started conducting meeting  in  every  section  president  in  Bauan  Public
Market.

      Tarpaulins, flyers  particularly  on  the  banning  of  plastics  were
distributed and posted at conspicuous  places  for  years  already  and  the
management said that they will continue to conduct IEC to all  stakeholders,
constituents in the barangays through the help  of  barangay  officials  and
other volunteers.

       The  management  commented   that   as   per   their   understanding,
requirements of COA Circular No. 2012-001 only applies for hauling  contract
for garbage collection and disposal. They said that they  do  not  have  any
contract agreement with any private entity on hauling wastes since they  are
using their own garbage collectors, drivers and LGU’s garbage  trucks.  They
only borrow trucks when theirs are not functional and  mentioned  that  they
will provide authorization certificate for those cases.

We recommended that:

   1. the Municipal  Treasurer  provide  the  Municipal  Accountant  at  the
      beginning of each year with a duly certified list showing the name  of
      taxpayers and the amount due and collectible  for the  year  as  basis
      for the setting-up of receivable; Likewise, submit to the  Sangguniang
      Bayan certified list of all  real  property  tax  delinquencies  which
      remained uncollected or unpaid  for  at  least  one  (1)  year  and  a
      statement of the reason or reasons for  such  non-collection  or  non-
      payment  on  or  before  December  thirty-first  (31st)  of  the  year
      immediately succeeding  the  year  in  which  the  delinquencies  were
      incurred, with a request for assistance  in  the  enforcement  of  the
      remedies for collection;

   2. the Municipal Accountant reconcile and ensure the proper recording  of
      transactions affecting the balances of  the  RPT  Receivable  and  SET
      Receivable which  were  supposedly  of  the  same  amount  since  each
      corresponds to 1% of the assessed value of real property;

   3. Municipal Accountant  and  the  Municipal  Treasurer  reconcile  their
      records to present an accurate and reliable RPT and SET Receivables in
      the financial statements; and

   4. Municipal Mayor, as  well  as  the  Sanggunian  Bayan  Members,  adopt
      measures to collect  from  delinquent  taxpayers  and  strengthen  and
      intensify  collection  system  and  tax  campaign  to   minimize   tax
      delinquencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:

a)  the  Municipal  Accountant  to  religiously  demand   for   the   prompt
   liquidation of their cash advances following the  prescribed  period  and
   ensure that all claims are completely supported.

b) the concerned personnel to refund the total  claims  of  ₱4,277.00  which
   are not allowed in audit.

c) the Municipal Treasurer and the  Municipal  Accountant  to  refrain  from
   granting cash advance to employees who are not designated  as  disbursing
   officers for purposes other than travel.

d)  the  concerned  officials   to   submit   explanation   on   the   noted
   inconsistencies on the dates of the supporting official receipts.

e) the  Municipal  Treasurer  and  the  Municipal  Accountant  to  stop  the
   practice of granting cash advances to elected officials when it is not to
   be used for traveling expenses as prescribed  in  Section  4.1.4  of  COA
   Circular 97-002.

Management Comment:

      The management agreed with the audit recommendation and  assured  that
the necessary action shall be taken to reconcile the  balances  of  the  RPT
and SET Receivables.

observations.recommendation:

Act.                   
Instruct the MPDC to

Encourage the four

Recovery Facility in

32 of the said Act.     
Enforce strictly all    
enacted ordinances
especially on the       
segregation of wastes
at source, by households
and different           
institutions and        
establishments.        
Instruct the barangay    
officials to appropriate
funds for solid waste    
management in their     
respective barangay.   
Continue the            
dissemination of        
information on           
ordinances adopted      
regarding solid waste   
management to various   
residents and           
establishments.
observations.managementAction:
Garbage fees are
being collected
from
establishments
upon application
of business
permits. It is
included in the
Revenue Code of
the
municipality.

For CY 2016,
income from
garbage fees
from
establishments
amounted to
Php2,597,800.84.

Environmental
Code of Bauan
Ordinance No.
14-01-0056
Series of 2014
has been
legislated and
approved by the
Sangguniang
Bayan Members
and creation of
MENRO was
among the
provision set
forth in it.
Since, the
municipality is
in adherence
with the
provision of
RA-9003, MENRO
was created and
positioned at
the start of
2016.

Barangays
Balayong and
Cupang are
included in the
collection
service of the
municipality.
Recyclables are
brought to
junkshops.
Recyclable
wastes are
stored at the
central MRF at
the landfill
upon disposal
since final
segregation is
being done
thereat.

Barangay Bagong
Silang,
however,
practices
traditional
composting at
their respective
backyards.

The management
commented that
upon collection
of wastes,
segregation was
done by the
garbage
collectors. Final
segregation was
done at the
central MRF at
the sanitary
landfill. No
mixed wastes
were stored at
the landfill.
There is a
composting area
at the landfill
wherein
biodegradable
wastes are
dumped. Compost
derived thereat
was utilized at
the eco garden.
Wastes from
industries are
not included in
the collection
service by the
LGU. Wastes
from industries
are properly
segregated since
they have multi
partite
monitoring (MMT)
system that
monitors their
compliance with
the
Environmental
Compliance
Certificate (ECC)
and wastes are
one of them.
They have DENR
accredited
haulers that
transport all
kinds of
segregated
wastes to
accredited
processors/treat
ers.

Brgys. Aplaya
and Balayong
which were of
the 13 barangay
previously
included in the
collection
service of the
municipality
have already
purchased their
own garbage
truck and are
not anymore
included in the
collection
service.

Barangays with
their own
garbage trucks
and other
barangays which
are included in
the collection
service of the
municipality
collect garbage
fees from each
household. The
proceed is being
utilized in the
hauling and
disposal of
wastes at the
landfill. Proceeds
from selling
Recyclable
wastes that
turned over to
junkshops are
also being
utilized. All
other
environment
activities are
funded with the
clean and green
appropriation.

Information
dissemination
was done thru
billboards,
tarpaulins and
dialogues with
the barangay
officials. There
were two
meetings
conducted/initi
ated by the
Sangguniang
Bayan during the
first quarter of
2016 with the
respective
department Heads
of the
municipality and
Market Vendors
Association for
the
implementation
of an ordinance
regarding
banning of
plastics and
proper waste
segregation. 
There were also
two occasions of
Association of
Barangay
Council’s
meeting wherein
they emphasized
the Solid Waste
Management in
the respective
barangays and
informed them of
the provision of
Basura Patroller
from DENR. They
also conducted
an Ecological
Summit last
December 2016
for all business
establishments
including
industries,
institutions to
provide
information on
the importance
of solid waste
management. DENR
personnel were
invited as
speakers.

While barangay
Durungao has
MOA with junkshop
for the
collection of
their recyclable
wastes. As per
management, the
practice is
acceptable with
the NSWMC-DENR.

No enough
budget available
for the purchase
of new garbage
trucks.

They have DENR
accredited
haulers that
transport all
kinds of
segregated
wastes.

Brgys. Aplaya
and Balayong
which were of
the 13 barangays
previously
included in the
collection
service of the
municipality
have already
purchased their
own garbage
truck and are
not anymore
included in the
collection
service.

The MENRO
together with
the Solid Waste
Management
Board will re-
assess on how
the set goals
and targets in
the 10 year
Solid Waste
Management Plan
will be 100%
attained. The
LGU said that
they will try
their best to
implement the
“no segregation
no collection
policy”. The LGU
will conduct IEC
to all
stakeholders for
them to
understand the
RA 9003.

The LGU had
conducted Eco-
Summit to all
establishment
located in
Bauan regarding
the implementation
of RA 9003 last
December 2016.

Tarpaulins, flyers
particularly on
the banning of
plastics were
distributed and
posted at
conspicuous
places for years
already and the
management said
that they will
continue to
conduct IEC to
all stakeholders,
constituents in
the barangays
